summary:
Cold hydrofluoric acid effectively cleans base metal alloys while minimizing damage to the underlying material; it offers better safety profiles than other chemical treatments or abrasive methods commonly used.
The primary method recommended for thoroughly cleansing base metal alloys involves using cold hydrofluoric acid because this approach ensures selective dissolution of surface contaminants without significant erosion of the metallic substrates, offering enhanced precision alongside improved operational safety relative to alternative treatment strategies such as strong acidic solutions at higher temperatures or mechanical abrasion processes utilizing materials like aluminum oxide.

Answer choice: B
